reason: Belle de jour offers an intimate, existential character study of mid-’60s Parisian malaise.
8 1/2 is an iconic ’60s Italian slice-of-life into a filmmaker’s existential crisis.
2001: A Space Odyssey meditates on purpose and humanity with cold, slice-of-life precision.
A Clockwork Orange probes morality and identity in a stylized, postmodern ’60s setting.
Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid deconstructs western myths with bittersweet character focus.
Bonnie and Clyde’s 1960s drama reframes outlaw romance as a tragic, human-driven study.
The Big Sleep’s cynical PI and mature pace make for an existential ’40s-style slice-of-life noir.
The Third Man’s post-war Vienna is a haunting backdrop to a masterful philosophical study.
Notorious examines personal conflict and loyalty in ’60s espionage with a subtle, human touch.
To Catch a Thief offers a refined ’60s character study of identity and intrigue in Riviera life.
